page: Amazon Web Services m8g.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by custom AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processors. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz, with 1 thread per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. This instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8g.4xlarge demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier Passmark memory score and strong uncached large-block memory bandwidth of 296.67 GB/sec for read operations and 133.88 GB/sec for write operations. Single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression also register strong results. General CPU performance is average to strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 15876.0 and a single-core score of 1883.0. However, the instance shows weaker performance in LLM inference prompt processing for medium-sized 7B models, registering 31.24 tokens/sec.

The instance is suitable for general-purpose workloads, including database operations, text processing, and software compilation. Its high memory bandwidth and dedicated core allocation provide reliable resource density for multi-threaded applications, though workloads requiring local NVMe storage or high-performance GPU acceleration will require alternative configurations.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by a 16-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 processor on the arm64 architecture. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 16 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz and 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with top-tier Passmark memory scores and strong uncached large-block memory read bandwidth of 296.67 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong multi-threaded decompression and database operation capabilities. The m8g.4xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, text processing, and database operations that do not require local SSD storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.4xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 64 GB RAM, and strong memory bandwidth, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.4xlarge is an arm64 general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high memory bandwidth for balanced, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 physical cores on arm64 architecture
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Strong memory bandwidth with 296.67 GB/sec uncached read performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth running on the Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database operations, text processing, and general-purpose workloads
